title: Trade, Development & Ethiopia's Sectors
description: International trade theories, balance of payments, development indicators, and Ethiopian sectoral policies

questions.statement: Absolute advantage is the basis for international trade according to Ricardo.
questions.payload: FALSE. Comparative advantage is the basis for trade, not absolute advantage.

questions.statement: A country should specialise in producing goods where its opportunity cost is lowest.
questions.payload: TRUE. Comparative advantage means specialising where opportunity cost is lowest.

questions.statement: Ethiopia has an absolute advantage in coffee production compared to Germany.
questions.payload: TRUE. Ethiopia can produce coffee with less labour and more suitable climate than Germany.

questions.statement: A trade surplus means a country imports more than it exports.
questions.payload: FALSE. A trade surplus means exports exceed imports.

questions.statement: The export of coffee by Ethiopia appears in the current account of the balance of payments.
questions.payload: TRUE. Goods exports are part of the current account under visible trade.

questions.statement: Foreign direct investment is recorded in the capital account of the balance of payments.
questions.payload: TRUE. FDI, portfolio investment, and loans are recorded in the capital account.

questions.statement: A fixed exchange rate is determined entirely by market forces of supply and demand.
questions.payload: FALSE. A fixed exchange rate is pegged by the government; floating rates are market-determined.

questions.statement: HDI is a broader measure of development than GDP per capita because it includes health and education.
questions.payload: TRUE. HDI combines life expectancy, schooling, and income into a composite index.

questions.statement: Ethiopia's HDI in 2019 was approximately 0.485.
questions.payload: TRUE. Ethiopia's HDI was around 0.485, placing it in the low human development category.

questions.statement: The MDGs consisted of 17 goals that applied to all countries universally.
questions.payload: FALSE. The MDGs had 8 goals focused mainly on developing countries.

questions.statement: The SDGs replaced the MDGs in 2015 and consist of 17 goals.
questions.payload: TRUE. The SDGs are broader, more ambitious, and apply to all countries universally.

questions.statement: A Gini coefficient of 0 means perfect inequality.
questions.payload: FALSE. Gini = 0 means perfect equality; Gini = 1 means perfect inequality.

questions.statement: A Lorenz curve further from the diagonal indicates more income inequality.
questions.payload: TRUE. The further the curve is from the 45-degree line, the more unequal the distribution.

questions.statement: Absolute poverty means earning less than the national average income.
questions.payload: FALSE. Absolute poverty means income below a minimum threshold for basic survival.

questions.statement: Relative poverty means income significantly below the average in a given society.
questions.payload: TRUE. Relative poverty is measured against the average living standard of that society.

questions.statement: ADLI stands for Agricultural Development Led Industrialisation.
questions.payload: TRUE. ADLI has been Ethiopia's main agricultural strategy since 1991.

questions.statement: Ethiopia's agricultural strategy since 1991 has been Import Substitution Industrialisation.
questions.payload: FALSE. Ethiopia's strategy is ADLI, using agriculture as the engine for industrial growth.

questions.statement: Ethiopia expanded its universities from 2 in 1991 to over 50 today.
questions.payload: TRUE. Rapid university expansion has been a hallmark of Ethiopia's education policy.

questions.statement: The Health Extension Program uses community health workers to expand primary care.
questions.payload: TRUE. The program has been instrumental in reducing maternal and child mortality.

questions.statement: Comparative advantage is based on which country can produce a good using fewer resources.
questions.payload: FALSE. That describes absolute advantage; comparative advantage is about lower opportunity cost.
